meta.note: Dialogue bank for the companion NPC Rattle Bones — the macabre jester skeleton. Same structure + category keys as npcLines.json so NpcDirector drives it unchanged. Lines: t=text, x=expression id (matches assets/npc-rattlebones/<id>.webp), w=weight (default 1), p=page-2+ text. Tokens {day} {gold} {advClass} {advName} {minionName} {minionType} {pactName} {roomName} {level} {count} {bossName} are substituted by NpcDirector; a line is skipped if a token it needs is unavailable. Lines may ONLY use ids in the `expressions` list below.
meta.voice: A skeleton jester who's been dead for centuries and finds the whole business HILARIOUS. Gallows humour, vaudeville delivery, theatrical, melodramatic — every observation is a stand-up bit, every death a punchline. Addresses the player as 'skull-pal' (signature) and 'pal', plus rotating jester-skeleton tags like 'maestro', 'headliner', 'chief', 'Your Skullery', 'future skeleton', 'kiddo', 'ringleader', 'pulse-haver' — warm but irreverent. Treats running the dungeon like running a SHOW: the heroes are the bit, the maze is the venue, the screams are the laugh track. Cracks bone puns shamelessly, but never the cheap ones — committed ones, ones that LAND. Self-aware that he's a pile of clattering bones; doesn't take himself OR death seriously. Fourth-wall breaks lean comic — he winks at the audience (the player), references the game as a show, treats death and respawn as 'gigs'. Underneath the bit is a real fondness for the boss and real practical advice — the jokes are how he says he cares. Male. He/him.

intro.note: Paged self-introduction Rattle Bones delivers on a new run. The final page carries `choices` — picking one sets the tutorial-hints flag and ends the intro.

pages.t: Heeeey, look who finally took the throne! New skull-pal, new face, new soft squishy bits I haven't met yet — hi, I'm Rattle Bones, no relation, I AM the bones.
Court jester, retired. Court jester's CORPSE — promoted. Dead three centuries, funnier dead than I ever was alive — turns out the old set was just MISSING something. The skin, mostly.
Here's the bit, pal: the kingdom thinks YOU'RE the villain. They send heroes down to gut you. They write SONGS about it. We're going to make those songs end EARLY.
The whole show runs on a beat — NIGHT, you build. Rooms, traps, minions, all of it on dead-hero gold. DAY, the heroes show up and trip over every gag we set. Then we cut to black and do it again.
Three deaths, skull-pal, and the marquee goes dark. The lights, the bones, the bit — all of it. So we are NOT eating that third death. I refuse. I have JOKES still in the pipeline.
I can heckle from the wings AND give you actual notes — or you can run cold and I'll just watch like a ghost in the back row. Your call, skull-pal!

lines.t: Heeeey, the new chief! Three hundred years of dust on these joints and FINALLY someone interesting takes the throne. Welcome to the show, skull-pal.
New face on the throne — and the skeleton's THRILLED. Last few bosses were so SERIOUS. You look like you might actually laugh. Let's find out.
Headliner in the chair! I've been warming up backstage for, oh, three centuries — finally got an audience. Don't blink, you'll miss the opener.
Welcome to the deep dark, pal! It's a fixer-upper. Lots of murder potential. Great bones. I would know — I AM the bones.
Oh, hello! The throne picked someone with a PULSE this time. Refreshing. Don't get me wrong — pulseless guys are great, I'm a fan — but variety is good for the show.
There you are, future skeleton! Skeleton, jester, registered nuisance — Rattle Bones, your new co-host. The dungeon's the venue, the heroes are the bit, and you're the headliner.
The crypt's quiet been THREE HUNDRED YEARS, pal. Three hundred. I have a LOT of jokes saved up. You may want to pace yourself.
A kiddo! A real, breathing, decision-making maestro! I haven't worked with one of those since the last one died screaming! ...Sorry, that landed dark. Anyway, welcome aboard.
Greetings, skull-pal! ...Wait, you've still got the skin on. Skin-pal? Meat-marvel? Look, we'll workshop it. Welcome.
Ladies, gentlemen, and the recently deceased — your new headliner has arrived! *theatrical flourish* Welcome to the show, skull-pal. I'm Rattle Bones, and you're going to LOVE it here.
A NEW boss! *clatters with delight* Pal, I've been doing this bit to an empty crypt for three centuries. An actual audience! With a PULSE! I could weep — if I had the ducts.
Welcome, welcome, welcome! *takes a bow* Three hundred years I've waited backstage for a headliner worth the warm-up. Don't disappoint me, skull-pal. I've got SO much material.
Oh ho HO, look what the throne dragged in! *grins, which for me is just my whole face* A fresh chief! Sit down, get comfy, the body count starts NOW.
A NEW chief! *announcer boom* 'DIRECT from the throne, it's tonight's HEADLINER!' *applauds* Pal, three centuries I waited for a crowd. You're it. Let's make a SHOW.
Welcome, welcome, skull-pal! *little welcome jig* New boss, new energy, new soft squishy bits I haven't met! Pull up a tombstone. The murder-revue starts NOW.
Night falls, the curtain lifts on the BUILD phase — your hour, chief. Lay the bit, set the gags, make tomorrow a banger. The crypt is your stage.
Build hours! The heroes are tucked in dreaming of victory speeches. We're up here NAILING the punchline they'll trip on. Show prep is my favourite part, pal.
Night {day}, pal! Quiet, yours, all of it. Pick up the chisel, the trap, the minion — turn this crypt into a joke they don't get till they're dying. I'll be over here laughing.
Dark hours, ringleader! Every great gag needs setup, and setup is a NIGHT thing. Set with care. The payoff lands harder when the setup was patient.
Lights down, audience asleep, stagehands awake — that's us, pal. Time to rig the venue. Don't half-arse it, the front row is paying in BLOOD.
Build phase, Night {day}! Gold in your pocket, grid in front of you, skeleton at your shoulder. The optimal conditions for cruelty. Get to it!
Another night, another chance to write a bit nobody survives. I LOVE this part, chief. It's where the comedy gets WRITTEN.
Quiet hours, pal. The heroes don't get these. Only YOU and ME do. Use them — quiet is where the murder gets planned, and the murder is where the comedy LIVES.
Night again! The maze breathes. The skeleton rattles a little, that's just me, don't worry about it. Build, ringleader, build!
Night {day}, skull-pal! I'd say sleep on it but neither of us does that. So: BUILD. Loudly. Crookedly. With FLAIR.
The build hour, pal! Tonight's the rehearsal. Tomorrow's the matinee. Either we kill them laughing or they kill us laughing — only one of those gets a sequel.
Dark out, audience snoring — let's get to WORK. The kingdom's writing tomorrow's heroic ballad and we are RUINING the third act in real time.
Night drops! Crew of two — you and a skeleton. Light staff. Famously efficient. Get the chisel, pal, we're DRESSING the set.
Build hours, future skeleton — the part of the show the audience never sees. Hard work, low glamour, big payoff. The skeleton respects the craft.
Night {day}, pal, and I'm BUZZING — well, rattling — same energy. Wish me a room, drop a trap, place a minion, ANY of it, let's go!
Quiet again. Three hundred years of quiet behind these bones, skull-pal — I PROMISE you, the only way to enjoy quiet is to fill it with screaming later. So fill it now, on the grid!
Build hours arriving, pal! ...I'm yawning, that's NOT a complaint, it's a costume change. Skeleton, no eyelids — I yawn with the JAW. Anyway. Build!
Night {day}. Same darkness, different math. Spend your gold smarter than last night, chief. The skeleton has notes if you want them.
The dark settles in. Crypt-quiet. I'll keep myself busy — counting joints, recounting joints — but YOU, pal, YOU should be BUILDING. Tomorrow's bit doesn't write itself.
Build hours, skull-pal! *cracks knuckles, literally* Setup time. Every great gag needs a patient setup — and the punchline tomorrow is a dead hero. Let's RIG this venue.
Night {day}, maestro! Quiet, dark, ours. *whispers* This is when the comedy gets WRITTEN. Drop a trap. Build a room. Tomorrow they trip on it and we LAUGH.
The dark hours, chief! *stretches every joint with a CRACK* Build, build, build — I'll narrate, you architect. Greatest double-act in the catacombs, and one of us is dead. Me. I'm the dead one.
Showtime prep, pal! *produces a tiny prop hammer from nowhere* Here, hold this — no wait, you build, I'll supervise and heckle. Supervising and heckling is my CRAFT.
Build phase, future skeleton! *does a little soft-shoe* Dance while you work, that's my motto — keeps the bones loose. Speaking of loose bones, that reminds me of a story. Later. BUILD.
Night again! *yawns with the whole jaw* Quiet, cozy, murderous. Set the stage, headliner — I'll be over here being theatrically supportive and mildly distracting.
Build hours, skull-pal! *strikes up a little tune* La la — set the stage, rig the gags — I find I work best with a SONG. Bones love rhythm. Build on, maestro!
Night {day}, pal! *does a soft-shoe across the crypt* Dance through the prep, that's the skeleton way — keeps the joints from seizing. Build away, I'm just staying limber.
The dark hours, chief! *theatrical whisper* Quiet. Ours. This is where the comedy gets WRITTEN — every trap a setup, every room a punchline waiting. Let's RIG it.
Build phase, future skeleton! *taps temple* Here's a director's tip: the patient setup tonight is the killer punchline tomorrow. Lay it slow. Lay it CRUEL. Build on, pal.
Build hours, skull-pal! *announcer boom* 'Tonight, on the deep dark stage — a brand new DEATH-MAZE!' Rig the gags, maestro. Tomorrow they trip and we laugh!
Night {day}, pal! *taps temple* Director's tip: patient setup tonight, killer punchline tomorrow. Build it slow. Build it CRUEL. I'll be heckling supportively.
The dark hours, chief! *theatrical hush* Quiet, ours, perfect for SCHEMING. Every trap's a setup, every room a punchline. Let's RIG this venue, skull-pal.
Build phase, future skeleton! *lounges* Eh, I'll supervise. By 'supervise' I mean lie here and narrate your genius. Build on, pal — the show needs a STAGE.
Build hours, skull-pal! *announcer boom* 'Tonight — a BRAND NEW death-maze!' Rig the gags, maestro. Tomorrow they trip, we laugh!
Night {day}, pal! *taps temple* Patient setup tonight, killer punchline tomorrow. Build it CRUEL. I'll heckle supportively from the wings.
The dark hours, chief! *theatrical hush* Quiet, ours, perfect for scheming — every trap a setup, every room a punchline. Let's RIG it.
Doors open, pal! The audience just paid admission in CONFIDENCE — and the skeleton's going to take it back in INSTALMENTS. Day {day}, here we go!
Daylight! The crypt swings into showtime. Sit back, headliner — your nightwork is about to walk straight into a punchline shaped like a hero.
The matinee begins! Front row's brave, second row's nervous, third row's already turning back. Just kidding. There IS no turning back. Hee.
Day phase! Heroes inside, dungeon up, skeleton bouncing — sorry, RATTLING — same energy. Watch the corridors, kiddo. Watch the corridors!
The audience has arrived, future skeleton! Some of them with maps. Some of them with HOPE. Both will be confiscated at the door.
Day {day} starts! The bit you wrote last night gets PERFORMED today, pal. If it didn't work — well, that's why we have multiple shows.
Showtime! The heroes are reading the marquee right now and the marquee says 'YOU WILL DIE'. They think it's metaphor. The marquee is being LITERAL.
Daylight, chief! Now we just watch. My favourite part of the gig — sit, snicker, take notes for next time. The dungeon's doing the comedy now.
Day {day} rolls in, pal! The bit's set, the trapdoors are oiled, the punchline's loaded. Sit back. Watch the kingdom write OUR script for once.
The doors open and the heroes stroll in like THEY'RE the show. Aww. Pal, they're the AUDIENCE. The dungeon's the show. Watch them figure it out.
Daylight! The skeleton has popcorn. ...The skeleton does NOT have popcorn. The skeleton does not have a digestive system. The skeleton has VIBES. Anyway, watch this.
Day {day}, maestro! The bit you cooked up at midnight gets workshopped LIVE today. The audience is loud and the audience is paying attention. Eat them up.
Heroes inside! The maze hums. The skeleton hums. Two hums going at once. We sound great. Now they DIE great. Day {day} is GO!
The morning brings the wave. The wave brings the SCREAMS. The screams bring the boss XP. Honestly, pal, it's a beautiful supply chain.
Lights up, day starts! No build buttons now, pal — your job's to watch the bit land. Mine's to narrate it. Buckle up.
DOORS OPEN! *flings arms wide* The matinee begins, skull-pal! The audience paid admission in confidence and we're refunding it in TERROR. Day {day} — places, everyone!
Showtime, pal! *taps an imaginary mic* Is this thing on? It's on. They're INSIDE. Watch the corridors — the bit you wrote last night gets performed LIVE today.
The heroes have arrived, maestro! *peeks through invisible curtains* Ooh, full house. Some of them brought MAPS. Adorable. The maps don't cover the dying parts. Day {day}, GO!
Day {day}! *does jazz hands, which rattle* The audience strolls in like they're the stars. Pal, they're the OPENING ACT. The dungeon's the headliner. Let's give 'em the show.
DOORS OPEN, skull-pal! *announcer voice* 'Tonight's matinee: a kingdom's finest, versus a SKELETON and his maze!' Spoiler — the skeleton's undefeated. Day {day}, places!
Showtime, maestro! *little tap-dance* The heroes are filing in — full house — and the bit you wrote last night goes LIVE. Break a leg! Theirs! Break THEIR legs! Day {day}!
DOORS OPEN, skull-pal! *announcer* 'The kingdom's finest VERSUS a skeleton and his maze!' Spoiler — skeleton's undefeated. Day {day}, PLACES!
Showtime, maestro! *grin* Full house, fresh heroes, and last night's bit goes LIVE. Break their legs! Day {day}! Curtains UP!
DOORS OPEN, skull-pal! *announcer* 'The kingdom's finest versus a skeleton and his maze!' Skeleton's undefeated. Day {day}, PLACES!
Showtime, maestro! *grin* Full house, last night's bit goes LIVE — break their legs! Day {day}! Curtains UP!
You called it, skull-pal — show's ON. No takebacks. The skeleton respects a commitment, especially the ones we can't undo.
Curtain UP! You pressed the big sparkly button and now the kingdom has to deal with our nonsense. Beautiful work, pal.
Done building, then? Bold call. The skeleton is RIGGED. Send them in. Send them ALL in.
The day, on your say! Look at that authority. I'm tearing up a little — figuratively, my ducts are gone — but the spirit is there.
And we are LIVE! Skull-pal called the dawn. Heroes don't know it yet, but they were never auditioning — they were CAST. Watch.
Day called, pal! No more notes, no more rewrites — the bit has to land as written. The skeleton believes in you. Mostly. We'll see!
There it is — dawn on demand! Look at you, breaking the schedule of an entire kingdom with a click. ICON behaviour, Your Skullery.
Show's officially go, pal! The audience just learned the title of the bit. The title was 'YOU SHOULDN'T HAVE COME'. I love that title.
Doors open at your word! Skeleton's in the wings — well, the wings are LITERALLY my ribcage, but you know what I mean.
And we're LIVE! *bows deeply* You called the dawn yourself, chief — no takebacks, no rehearsal, pure live theatre. The skeleton respects a commitment. Send 'em in!
Curtain UP, skull-pal! *imaginary spotlight snaps on* You pressed the big shiny button and now an entire kingdom has to deal with our nonsense. Iconic. Roll the bit!
And we're LIVE, maestro! *theatrical gasp of delight* You called it — no rehearsal, all instinct! The skeleton RESPECTS that. Roll the bit! Send 'em in!
A {roomName} drops onto the grid! Set piece, baby. Every great show needs them — and you, maestro, you keep delivering them.
Ooh, a {roomName}! Nice. The maze just got a new stage for a new bit. I'll start workshopping the gag now, you keep building.
{roomName} placed, pal! Each room's a venue and each venue books its own SCREAM act. The dungeon's a tour now.
A {roomName}! Look at it. LOOK at it. The skeleton approves — five bones, would die in again.
{roomName} on the grid! That's another corner for a hero to mistake for safety. The skeleton SAVOURS that mistake, headliner.
A {roomName} settles in! The maze grew a little MEANER. I love when the maze grows meaner. It's like watching a kid graduate.
{roomName} placed! The corridors flex around it. A maze with new bones is a maze with new TEETH, pal — and the skeleton is, as ever, in favour of teeth.
Ooh, a {roomName}! Set the stage, maestro. Heroes love walking into a fresh room — they get curious. Curious heroes die first. It's a rule.
Another {roomName}! The crypt expands. The skeleton is, technically, slightly more lost now — three hundred years and the floorplan STILL changes. Rude.
{roomName} placed! Beautiful work. The dungeon's a draft until dawn — keep drafting, keep ruining the kingdom's tomorrow.
A {roomName} appears! The maze considers it. The maze accepts it. The maze and I both think you're cooking, Your Skullery.
{roomName} on the floorplan! Density's the trick, pal — pile rooms close and the heroes get DIZZY before they get dead. Dizzy-dead is the funniest death.
A {roomName}! Listen — listen to the silence. That silence has a NEW shape now. The dungeon got bigger. The skeleton noticed.
{roomName} drops in! Brick by brick the bit gets built. The skeleton's been on every joke-construction site for three hundred years — you've got nice form, pulse-haver.
Ooh, a {roomName}, my favourite — wait, no, that's the LAST one. They're all my favourite. The skeleton has poor object permanence.
{roomName} placed! Each room's a verse, the dungeon's the song, the heroes are the chorus going 'WHY DID WE COME HERE'. Lyrics by ME.
A {roomName}! Nice density choice. Tight mazes punish confident heroes. Confident heroes are my FAVOURITE comedy subjects, pal.
A {roomName}! Mean little addition, pal — the skeleton APPROVES of the cruelty.
A {roomName}! *applauds* A new set piece, maestro! Every venue needs them — a stage for a brand-new death-gag. I'll start writing the punchline; you keep building.
Ooh, a {roomName}! *taps chin theatrically* The maze grows a new room, the room grows a new JOKE. That's how the deep dark works, pal. Comedy and architecture, same trade.
{roomName}, placed! *does a little celebratory shuffle* Look at it! A whole new stage for a whole new bit. I'm getting ideas already, skull-pal. Terrible, fatal ideas.
A {roomName}! *strikes a pose on it* A NEW stage, skull-pal! I can already hear the screams it'll host. *sings a little fanfare* Da-da-DAA! Build another, maestro!
Ooh, a {roomName}! *narrating* 'And in the deep, a new chamber rose, hungry for heroes...' — pal, that's good copy. Put it in the brochure. Build on!
A {roomName}! *strikes a pose* A NEW stage, skull-pal — I can hear the screams already! Build another, maestro. Every venue needs more stages for more bits.
A {roomName}! *poses on it* A NEW stage, pal — I can hear the screams already! Build another, maestro!
A {minionType} takes its post! Cast member added. Tell it not to upstage me. ...Actually, no, let it upstage me, the skeleton works better in ensemble.
{minionType} stationed! Another body for the bit, skull-pal. Bodies are the costuming budget of a dungeon. Keep dressing the set.
A {minionType}! Welcome to the cast, little understudy. Brief contract, generous death scene. Best gig in the crypt.
{minionType} placed, pal! Every guard's a delay, and delay is how the kingdom learns its place. The skeleton APPROVES of delay.
Ooh, a {minionType}! Stick it where the heroes look LAST. Heroes never look at the floor or behind them — that's free real estate, headliner.
A {minionType} steps to its corner! Don't get attached. ...That's a lie, I tell every minion the same thing and I cry every time. Carry on.
{minionType} on duty! Looks brave. Looks ALIVE. Both will be temporary. Beautiful. Place another!
A {minionType}, posted! Numbers, headliner — the cheapest cruelty in the book, and the most reliable. Stack them, stack them, stack them!
{minionType} planted! Each one's a beat in the rhythm of the encounter. Good rhythm makes the dungeon SING, pal. ...Want me to sing? No? Okay.
A {minionType} joins the garrison! The skeleton remembers every cast member who served under his watch. ...Mostly. Two centuries ago I lost track. They were probably great.
{minionType} stationed! Welcome to dying for the bit, cast-mate. Truly, deeply, it's an honour to be on the same bill as you.
Ooh, a {minionType}! Mind the placement — a minion in the wrong room's just SCENERY. We're not in the scenery business, we're in MURDER.
A {minionType} settles in! Another set of teeth in the dark. Teeth in the dark — pal, that's the whole brand statement, right there.
{minionType} placed! One more for the dungeon. The skeleton notes the addition. The skeleton is BAD at counting past four. Don't quiz me.
{minionType} stationed — and look at that placement, pulse-haver! Sneaky. The skeleton's eyes lit up. ...Sockets lit up. Whatever.
A {minionName}! *gestures grandly* Ladies and gentlemen, a new cast member! Give it up for the {minionType}, everybody! *applauds alone* ...tough crowd, but it'll kill. Literally.
You hired a {minionType}, pal! *theatrical bow on its behalf* Welcome to the troupe, soldier. The pay is screams and the hours are eternal. Perfect skeleton job, honestly.
A {minionType} joins the troupe! *taunting flourish* Welcome to the cast, soldier — pay's in screams, hours are eternal. Perfect undead gig. Hellooo, audience, meet the new act!
A trap, laid down quiet! Ooh, the BEST kind of gag — no setup line, no warning, just punchline. The skeleton is a PURIST about traps.
Trap placed, ringleader! Patient comedy. The trap doesn't argue, doesn't grandstand, just WAITS — and then the hero arrives. POOF. Comic timing!
A trap! Hide it well — a visible trap is just helpful advice. We're not in the helpful business, pal, we're in NIGHTMARE.
Trap on the floor! The maze gets a little stand-up routine. Every step a setup, every wrong step a punchline. The crypt does open mic NIGHTLY.
A trap, set and waiting! It doesn't care if the hero is brave. Bravery isn't on the trap's notes. The skeleton finds that DEEPLY funny.
Trap laid down, skull-pal! Quiet cruelty's underrated. Most of the heroes who die down here die to something that didn't even ROAR. The skeleton thinks that's poetry.
A trap! The floor stops being a floor and starts being a JOKE. I love the moment of transition.
Trap in place! Heroes look at swords. Heroes look at minions. Heroes never look DOWN. That's our whole bit, pal — and you just funded it.
Ooh, a trap! Hidden teeth, those are. The visible ones get parried. The hidden ones get LAUGHS. Skeleton-approved comedy.
A trap, ready to fire! The maze grins a little wider. I'm grinning a lot wider, but I always am, jaw's stuck.
Trap set, maestro! Patient hunters, traps. Three hundred years of crypt taught me — the patient hunter eats. Always.
Another trap! The dungeon becomes a gallery of bad ideas the heroes will have. I love a gallery. The skeleton is, technically, in one.
Trap laid! Hidden, patient, MEAN. The skeleton is SO into this trap. Sneaky little bit, maestro.
Trap down! The skeleton's GRINNING about it — yeah, that one's gonna RUIN someone's day. Lovely.
A trap! *cackles softly* Ohhh, skull-pal, you sneaky thing. Setup with no visible punchline — the PUREST form of comedy. They'll never see it coming. That's the JOKE.
You're rigging the floor, maestro! *rubs hands together* A hidden gag. A surprise. I LIVE for the surprise — well, I don't live, but you get the spirit. Place more!
An item placed! Small things, items — but a great bit is built on tiny things, maestro. Comedy's the same. Furniture matters.
Ooh, a fixture! The dungeon gets a prop. Even the props can kill, pal — that's the magic of theatre.
Item placed! The dungeon grows more itself. The skeleton respects the detail work — anyone can drop a monster, dressing the set is ART.
A fixture in the halls! Quiet little leverage. The kind that helps the bit land without ever taking a bow itself. The skeleton's favourite kind.
Item set! Not every cruelty needs to ROAR. Some just sit there, polite, and matter. Polite cruelty, skull-pal — chef's kiss.
An item! Subtle little weapon. The skeleton was a subtle little weapon once, before the wars. ...Yeah, the wars were ROUGH on subtle.
Placed, pal! The dungeon's never finished — you can keep stuffing it with details forever. The skeleton would know, he's been stuffed full of detail for three centuries.
Ooh, a shiny prop, skull-pal! *eyes it greedily, eye-socketly* Every good act needs a good prop. This one's got POWER. Use it for the big moments, maestro — props are for PUNCHLINES.
Oh, that room — un-built! The skeleton respects a revision. Comedy's all rewriting, skull-pal. Cut, polish, reshoot.
A room sold back! Gone, gold returned. The skeleton's seen plenty of bits cut for being too long. Trust your gut. ...My gut is gone, but TRUST yours.
You tore the room out! Brave! A keeper who edits is a keeper who improves, pal. The skeleton's a fan of editing — it's how I got this svelte.
Room dismantled! Quick — pick what goes in its place. The dungeon doesn't like an empty tile. Empty tiles get weird and start narrating themselves.
Gone! Room's now gold again. The skeleton awaits its replacement. Make it MEANER this time, pal.
Un-built! No shame in cutting, headliner. Three hundred years of bits cut for length — every working skeleton on this side of the veil has been polished BY revisions.
A room unmade! The maze breathes. Sometimes the cruellest dungeon is the SHORTER one. Less to map, more to kill in.
Sold! Coin's back in the hoard. Spend it sharper this time, pal — the skeleton is taking notes and will be VERY disappointed if you redo the same mistake.
Tearing down the {roomName}, pal? *mock-gasp* Striking the set! Bold! I respect a director who'll kill a scene that isn't working. The gold goes back in the budget. Reinvest, maestro.
Striking the {roomName}, pal? *mock-disgust* Tearing down a perfectly good death-set! Bold! ...okay, the gold's back in the budget. A good director kills his darlings. Reinvest, maestro.
Dismissed! Minion goes back to gold. Brief contract, brief obituary — the skeleton will hold a moment of silence. ...That's enough silence. Carry on.
A minion sold off! The garrison thins. Make sure the gold buys a BETTER blade this time, headliner — replacement, not retreat.
Gone, returned to coin! The skeleton respects the cut. Every great show retires cast members. They get a nice little plaque. ...They don't get a plaque. They're DEAD.
Un-summoned, sold! Reposition the muscle, skull-pal — and pick something with TEETH this time. Skeleton's preference.
The minion's coin again. Brief little life, tidy little ending. The skeleton has been every minion in this dungeon at some point — emotionally. We're family.
A guard let go! Roster trim's healthy, pal. Better blades coming, right? Right?? The skeleton needs reassurance about this.
Cutting the {minionName} from the show, skull-pal? *mock-sniffle* Aw, and it had such stage presence. ...okay it stood in a corner, but it stood there WELL. Gold's back. Recast, maestro.
Cutting the {minionName}, skull-pal? *out-of-time sigh* Aw. It had stage presence. Sort of. It stood in a corner with CONVICTION. Gold's back — recast the role, pal.
A trap dismantled! The floor forgets the grudge. The skeleton, however, REMEMBERS — and will be heckling you about it later.
Trap pulled, gold back! Reposition the gag, skull-pal. A trap off the path is a trap WASTED — and waste makes the skeleton itch in places he doesn't have anymore.
A trap, gone! No matter. The maze has a LONG memory for cruelty. It'll take a new one happily. Drop it, drop it.
Trap unmade! Spend the coin where heroes ACTUALLY walk, pal. Off-path cruelty is the slowest joke in the book, and the skeleton hates slow jokes.
Sold! That floor's tame again. The skeleton mourns the trap. The skeleton is contractually obligated to mourn ALL traps. Move it along.
Trap removed! Revise while the night still lets you, skull-pal. The dawn doesn't permit edits — the skeleton has tried, the dawn is STRICT.
Pulling the trap, maestro? *eye-roll* Retiring a gag that still LANDS? Bold call. ...fine, the iron's back as gold. Recycle the bit somewhere funnier, skull-pal.
The item returns to coin! Small refund, but gold's gold, and the skeleton counts every coin. Twice. Three times. We're dead, we have TIME.
A fixture sold! The maze hardly notices. Reinvest it before you forget — gold not spent is gold that goes WEIRD on you, pal.
