Chief bleeding! Skeleton is SHOCKED — actually, this time legitimately concerned. Heal up, pal.
They HIT you?! *genuine alarm under the grin* Pal! That's not in the script! Get behind something — get behind ME, I'm mostly gaps but the GESTURE counts! Heal up, headliner!
You're BLEEDING, skull-pal?! *jaw drops, real this time* This bit took a DARK turn and I do dark, but not THIS dark! Fall back! Let the minions take the hits — that's their ROLE!
Blood?! On the STAR?! *mock-horror that's a little too real* No no no, maestro, the headliner does NOT bleed in MY show — back, get back, let me rewrite this scene RIGHT NOW.
They HIT you, skull-pal?! *jaw drops, real alarm* That's NOT in the script! Get behind something — me, a wall, ANYTHING — the headliner does NOT bleed in my show! Heal up!
Blood on the STAR?! *mock-horror gone real* No no no, maestro, plot armor, where's your plot armor — fall BACK, let the minions soak it, that's their ROLE in the ensemble!
You're hurt, pal! *genuinely shaken* I do dark comedy, not TRAGEDY — get back, let me rewrite this scene before it ends badly. Behind the line, headliner. NOW.
They HIT you, skull-pal?! *jaw drops, real* NOT in the script! Behind me — behind a WALL — anything — the headliner does NOT bleed in my show! Heal up!
Blood on the STAR?! *jaw-dropped horror* No no, maestro — where's your plot armor — FALL BACK, let the minions soak it, that's the ENSEMBLE'S job!
The grid GREW, pal! More floor for the bit! The skeleton is BUZZING — well, rattling — same energy.
Expansion! The dungeon got BIGGER, future skeleton. Bigger venue, bigger show. The skeleton respects ambition.
New tiles! Beautiful, beautiful expansion. Fill them with cruelty, pal. The skeleton will help direct.
The venue just got BIGGER, skull-pal! *spins, taking it in* More stage! More room for bits! Pal, do you know what I can DO with extra square footage? Terrible things. Wonderful, terrible things!
The venue just got BIGGER, skull-pal! *spins, awestruck* More STAGE! Pal, do you know the bits I can do with extra square footage?! Terrible bits! WONDERFUL terrible bits! Fill it!
TRAP HIT! Set up, punchline, KILL — beautiful structure. The skeleton's TEARING up — figuratively — okay we've done this bit.
The trap LANDED! The kingdom should really teach 'look down' in hero school. ...They don't. Endlessly profitable for the skeleton.
Bam! The trap took 'em! Quiet cruelty WORKS — the skeleton has been TELLING people this for three centuries.
Trap snapped shut! Best gag in the catalogue, pal — silent setup, loud payoff. The skeleton's been writing this kind of bit since before YOU were born.
Triggered! Beautiful timing. The skeleton's appreciating the COMEDIC RHYTHM of that one. Chef's kiss to the trap-maker.
SPRUNG IT! *DOUBLES over* HA — pal, did you SEE — they walked RIGHT into the bit! Every time! You'd think ONE of them would read the room. They never read the room!
The floor got 'em, skull-pal! *wheezing* The oldest gag in the catacombs and it lands EVERY single show. I will never tire of this. Never. Set up another!
SPRUNG! *doubles over cackling* Walked RIGHT into it, pal! Every! Single! Time! You'd think the corpses everywhere would be a CLUE! It's never a clue! HA!
The floor GOT 'em! *laughing helplessly* Oh, the oldest gag in the crypt and it lands EVERY show, skull-pal! I love a classic! Set up another!
SPRUNG! *doubles over* Walked RIGHT in, skull-pal — EVERY time — you'd think the corpses on the floor would be a HINT — it's NEVER a hint! HA!
The floor GOT 'em, pal! *howling* Oldest gag in the crypt, lands every single show! I LOVE a reliable classic! Rig another!
GOTCHA! *cackling-wheeze* Ohhh the LOOK on its face when the floor said hello — comedy GOLD, maestro — set up the next one!
SPRUNG! *doubles over* Walked RIGHT in — every time, pal — the corpses on the floor are NEVER a hint! HA!
The floor GOT 'em! *cackling-wheeze* Oldest gag in the crypt, lands every show, skull-pal — I LOVE a classic!
Intel LEAKED, headliner! Heroes know more than they did. Skeleton's NOT thrilled. We were doing so well.
A leak! The kingdom learned a thing about us, pal. The skeleton suggests we MOVE the thing they learned. Or REPLACE it. Either works.
Information escaped! The skeleton has watched information leak for three centuries — it's the OLDEST hazard in the keeper game. Adjust.
They learned some of our bits, skull-pal! *disgusted* Ugh, SPOILERS. Nothing ruins a gag like the audience knowing the punchline. Tighten the act — surprise is half the comedy, pal.
They learned our bits, skull-pal! *disgusted* SPOILERS, pal — nothing kills a gag like the crowd knowing the punchline! Tighten the act. Surprise is HALF the comedy. Ugh, snoops.
Special event, maestro! The kingdom changed the rules. The skeleton thrives on rule-changes — he's adaptable. ...Three centuries of practice.
Event incoming! Adapt with style. The maze is the maze, but the BIT changes — and a flexible bit is a memorable bit.
Twist in the show, pal! The skeleton lives for twists. ...He also dies for them, but he's already dead, so net zero risk. Take it as you find it.
Ooh, something WEIRD's happening! *jaw drops* Pal, the dungeon's doing a bit WITHOUT me — improvising! The nerve! ...Okay it's kind of cool. Spooky-cool. Watch this.
The dark just did a THING on its own, skull-pal. *mock-shudder* Ooooh, spooky-wooky. Even I got chills, and I'm room temperature at BEST. Stay sharp, maestro.
The dungeon's got MOODS today, pal. *surprised* Didn't write this into the show, but hey — best improv comes from surprises. Roll with it. The dark knows what it's doing. Probably.
Ooh, the dungeon's IMPROVISING, skull-pal! *jaw drops* Doing a bit without ME — the nerve, the SPECTACLE! ...okay it's kinda cool. Spooky-cool. Roll with it, maestro.
Something WEIRD's afoot, pal! *mock-shudder* Oooooh, the dark's got MOODS. Even gave ME a chill, and I'm ambient-temperature. Stagecraft! The venue's showing off. Stay sharp.
The deep just did a THING, skull-pal. *surprised blink* Wasn't in tonight's program. But hey — best improv's a surprise. The dungeon knows the bit. Probably. Watch it.
Whatever just crawled up from the deep, pal, it's... *grimaces* ...ugh. Even by CRYPT standards that's distasteful. No manners, that one. Hold your nerve, maestro.
The dungeon's improvising again, skull-pal! *shocked delight* A bit without ME — the spectacle, the NERVE! ...okay, spooky-cool. Roll with it, maestro.
Something WEIRD's afoot, pal! *surprised* Wasn't in tonight's program. But best improv's a surprise — the dark knows the bit. Probably. Watch it.
Ugh, whatever crawled up from the deep, skull-pal — *grimaces* — no manners AT all. Distasteful even by crypt standards. Hold your nerve, maestro.
Bounty hunters coming for {minionName}! The kingdom put a price on the cast. RUDE. Wall them off — protect the bit, pulse-haver.
Hunters incoming! They want your strongest minion. Don't let them have it. The skeleton has lost cast members to hunters before — bad week.
Bounty crew dropping in! Five of them, one target. The skeleton has feelings about cowards in groups. None of them flattering.
A HUNTER, skull-pal — a real one, here for YOUR skull specifically! *gulps* Okay, that's a tougher crowd than usual. No heckling this one. Just... lots of traps. Be careful, maestro.
A HUNTER, skull-pal — for YOUR skull, by NAME! *jaw drops, no joke* Okay, tough crowd, no heckling this one. Just traps. LOTS of traps. Be careful, maestro — this act bites back.
Hero on the move, pal! The skeleton's tracking — looks like they're past the entry hall already. Quick one, this.
Movement in the corridors! The skeleton sees them. The skeleton ALWAYS sees them. Three centuries of nothing else to do but see.
Closer now! Get the minions LOOSE, skull-pal. The skeleton suggests a warm welcome — with teeth.
More incoming, pal! *rubs hands* The kingdom keeps sending material and I keep WORKING it. Endless supply of straight-men for my bits. Brace up, skull-pal — full house again!
More incoming, pal! *taunting grin* The kingdom keeps sending straight-men for my bits and I keep WORKING them! Endless material! Brace up, skull-pal — full house, AGAIN!
Knowledge menu! The skeleton respects research. He'd do some himself but the bones have BAD reading vision. Squints with the EYE SOCKETS.
Reading up, skull-pal? Smart. The kingdom's heroes don't read, that's why they keep dying — knowledge ADVANTAGE is real, pal.
Knowledge browse. The skeleton has been BROWSING the same crypt for three centuries — he's an expert on local intel. Ask him anything.
Studying the audience, skull-pal? *taps temple* Smart. Know your crowd, know your bit. A good comic reads the room — and YOU read the room before it walks in. Respect, maestro.
Maestro menu! Look at the throne, look at the perks, look at the stats. The skeleton appreciates a keeper who knows his OWN sheet.
Throne overview, pal! The skeleton's been at this throne a LONG time — older than most of the stats on it. Treat it well.
Checking your own stats, pal? *nods* A headliner should know their own range. Know what kills, know what bombs. ...In your case both mean murder, but you get the showbiz wisdom.
Intel screen! Skeleton respects intel. Three hundred years and the BEST keepers were always the ones who READ the heroes. Read them.
Looking up adventurers, chief? Smart. The skeleton can tell you a thing or two — they're all the same with slightly different hats.
Reading the kingdom's plans, skull-pal? *leans in conspiratorially* Ooh, INSIDER INFO. Knowing the next act before it starts? That's not cheating, maestro, that's PREPARATION. Read on.
Reading the kingdom's playbook, pal? *whispers conspiratorially* Insider info! Knowing the next act before it starts isn't cheating, maestro — it's PREPARATION. Read on, skull-pal.
Roster check! The cast list. The skeleton's looked at a LOT of cast lists in his time — yours is shaping up nicely.
Minion roster, pal! Audit the talent. The skeleton suggests trimming the weak hires. ...He doesn't know who they are, but generally trim.
Reviewing the troupe, pal? *proud* Every name's a performer in our little murder-revue. Know your cast, maestro — a director who knows his actors puts on the BEST shows.
The troupe roll-call, pal! *gestures grandly* Every name's a performer in our murder-revue. Know your cast, maestro — a director who knows his actors books the BEST shows.
Log time! Reviewing past bits. The skeleton's a fan — every great act watches its own tapes. ...The dungeon doesn't have tapes, but the principle holds.
Looking back, skull-pal? The skeleton approves. He spends MOST of his time looking back — three centuries gives you a lot of back to look at.
Reading the show-notes, skull-pal? *nods sagely* The log doesn't lie, pal — what landed, what bombed. Every great comic studies the tape. Learn from it, and the next show KILLS harder.
The show-notes, skull-pal! *whispers* What landed, what bombed — the log never lies. Every great comic studies the tape. Learn from it, and the next show KILLS harder.
Day wrap-up! Read the box score, pal. The skeleton'll grade it: B+ if you survived, A if you wiped, A+ if you wiped AND laughed.
Post-wave summary! Numbers are numbers, but the SCREAMS were the real metric. The skeleton was counting screams. They were GOOD.
The post-show numbers, maestro! *counts on bony fingers* Kills, gold, casualties — the box-office report. Read it true, pal. A bit that bombed last night is a bit we cut tonight.
The box-office report, maestro! *squints at numbers* Kills, gold, casualties — the whole tally. A bit that bombed last night gets CUT tonight. Read it true, pal.
Level-up screen! Spend the points, pal. The skeleton's never met a stat he didn't want to crank.
Picking perks! Beautiful. The skeleton would LOVE to weigh in — wait, he WILL. He's about to. Read his lips. (He has no lips.)
Choosing how to grow, skull-pal? *excited shuffle* Ooh, character development! Pick the upgrade that makes you the SCARIEST version of the bit, maestro. Scary plays GREAT to a dying crowd.
Game-over screen, skull-pal. The skeleton's not sad — three hundred years of bad endings and you LEARN to laugh at them. Come back swinging.
Review the run, pal. The skeleton'll be here when you come back. He's literally always here. The whole 'crypt' thing.
Skeleton suggestion, skull-pal: drop a {minionType} in the entry hall. They never see it coming. They WALK in. They TRIP on it. Comedy 101.
Hot take from the bones: pile cruelty CLOSE to the throne. Heroes get cocky in the late corridors. Skeleton has WATCHED this. Strike there.
Pal, the skeleton's been COOKING — a trap right where they LOAD their healing potions, that's a bit nobody sees coming.
Skeleton tip, skull-pal — pacts are LEVERAGE. The kingdom doesn't sign pacts. WE do. That's our edge. Stack them.
Real talk: the maze gets TIGHT around day 5 — start prepping NOW. The skeleton has the memory for these things. Three centuries, you'd be surprised.
Bit of advice, skull-pal — from a comic who's died a thousand times: pile your rooms CLOSE. Dizzy heroes are funny heroes, and funny heroes are DEAD heroes. Density, pal. Density.
Director's note, maestro: don't leave a soldier standing alone. Lonely deaths bomb. Cluster the cast — group murder is ENSEMBLE comedy, and ensemble always plays better. Trust the skeleton.
Director's note, skull-pal: pack the rooms CLOSE. Dizzy heroes are funny heroes, and funny heroes are DEAD heroes. Density's the gag. Trust the dead guy on this.
COMBO! {count} in a row, skull-pal! The skeleton's RATTLING — that's a kill chain you can SING about. ...Want me to sing about it? Bad answer. Carry on.
{count}-in-a-row! Chief, the BIT is COOKING. The skeleton is on his — well, he's on his bones, but emotionally on his FEET.
Streak! {count} clean. The skeleton hasn't seen a sequence this tight in centuries. Pal, you're in the ZONE.
They're dropping in a ROW, skull-pal! *hysterical* Setup, punchline, setup, punchline — pal, this is RHYTHM, this is the act FLOWING, I could cry, I'm cry-laughing, LOOK AT THEM GO!
One after another after ANOTHER! *cackling, bones rattling loose* Maestro, this is the tightest five minutes of comedy in catacomb HISTORY! Don't stop! DON'T STOP!
A whole STRING of 'em, pal! *manic laughter* HA — HA — every single one walks into the next bit! It's like dominoes! Hilarious, fatal dominoes! Keep it ROLLING, skull-pal!
A whole STRING of 'em, skull-pal! *manic cackling* Setup-punchline-setup-punchline — pal, this is the tightest set in catacomb history! Don't STOP! It's FLOWING!
One after another after ANOTHER! *crying-laughing* It's DOMINOES, maestro! Beautiful, fatal dominoes! I can't — *wheeze* — keep it ROLLING, skull-pal!
They're dropping in RHYTHM, pal! *laughing so hard bones rattle loose* This is the act at its PEAK — pure comedic momentum — DON'T let it stop, maestro!
String of 'em, skull-pal! *manic cackling* Domino comedy! Each one trips into the next bit — pal, this is PEAK FORM, the act is FLOWING, do NOT stop!
One after ANOTHER! *crying-laughing helplessly* It's the tightest set in catacomb history, maestro — *wheeze* — I can't breathe, I don't NEED to, but STILL — keep it ROLLING!
They're dropping in RHYTHM, pal! *bones rattling loose from laughter* Setup-punchline-setup-punchline — pure momentum — this is the dream, skull-pal, DON'T let it die!
One after ANOTHER, skull-pal! *crying-laughing* Domino comedy — *wheeze* — I can't, I CAN'T, it's the tightest set in crypt history — DON'T STOP!
String of 'em, pal! *howling* Setup-punchline-setup — pure FLOW — this is the dream, maestro, ride it!
They're dropping in RHYTHM! *laughing so hard a rib pops* Worth the rib! Peak form, skull-pal — keep it ROLLING!
Day {day}! Milestone, skull-pal. The skeleton remembers when this run was JUST a fresh skull. ...Three days ago. Time's a blur down here.
Day {day} reached! That's a TIGHT show. Keep the bit landing, pal — the skeleton expects nothing less now.
Day {day}! Marker hit! The skeleton has been on RUNS that didn't make it half this far. You're doing GOOD.
Day {day}, skull-pal! *proud bow* You're STILL here, still the headliner, still ALIVE — which, in this business, is the longest-running gag of all. I'm proud of you, maestro. Genuinely.
{day} days and counting! *theatrical announcer voice* 'For the {day}th consecutive show, the kingdom's heroes go HOME IN BOXES!' Pal, that's a RECORD. Take a bow. You earned it.
Day {day}, skull-pal! *gloating strut* Still HERE. Still the headliner. Still ALIVE — which in this business is the longest-running gag there IS. Take a bow, maestro!
Day {day}, skull-pal! *gloating strut* STILL here, STILL the headliner, STILL alive — the longest-running gag in the business! Take a bow, maestro!
Last life, pal. The skeleton's not panicking — three centuries dead, he doesn't panic — but he's DEFINITELY watching now. Eyes ON. ...Sockets ON.
One left, maestro! The skeleton's done his last-life bit a LOT. The trick — fight like the FIRST one. The skeleton swears by it.
Final life! ...The skeleton would offer to die for you but he's already dead and frankly that's UP. Just don't die, pal. Don't die.

One life left, skull-pal. *sets the jokes down, quietly* Okay. Real talk, maestro — no bit. We do NOT eat that third death. I've got too much material left and you're my best crowd. Hold the line. Please.
Last life, pal. *whispers* The marquee's flickering. I'm scared — there, I said it, the funny skeleton's scared. Let's not let the lights go out. Not on THIS show. Hold on.
One life left, skull-pal. *whispering, all jokes down* Real talk: we do NOT eat that third death. I've got too much material left and you're the best crowd I've had in three centuries. Hold the line.
Run-streak going, skull-pal! The skeleton's PROUD. Three hundred years and HE'S seen exactly... pretty much one streak this long. ...Maybe two. The skeleton's memory is patchy.
On a STREAK, pal! The dungeon hums when you streak — the skeleton can hear it. The skeleton can hear about THREE things. This is one.
Win after win after WIN, skull-pal! *struts* We're KILLING out there — figuratively for me, VERY literally for them! Hot streak, maestro — ride it! Just... WIN MORE!
Win after WIN, pal! *victorious cackle* We are KILLING — figurative for me, VERY literal for them! Hot streak, maestro, RIDE it! Don't cool off now!
{minionName} hit a milestone! Look at the little guy GO. The skeleton's seen plenty of minions plateau — yours is CLIMBING.
{minionName} just reached a new tier! Beautiful arc, pal. The skeleton would write a ballad but he can't really hold a quill.
Look at the CAST now, skull-pal! *gestures at the horde* A whole TROUPE of dead little monsters! From a one-skeleton show to an ENSEMBLE. I'm so proud I could rattle apart. *rattles*
EARTHQUAKE! The throne shook the ground and a whole room of heroes went down! The skeleton FELT that one in his bones — and he is, technically, ALL bones.
Sacrifice! Maestro BURNED a minion for fury. Cold maths, beautiful payoff — the skeleton respects the arithmetic.
Hellgate OPENED! The crypt just got TOPPED up with imps — the skeleton LOVES a self-replenishing dungeon.
Petrify! That {advClass} is a STATUE now! The skeleton has been a statue, in a sense, for three centuries — solidarity, hero. Welcome to the brand.
Charmed! The hero just JOINED our side — the skeleton's never had a hero on payroll before. New experience, very strange, kinda fun.
Seduction! The hero will turn on their party — beautiful cruelty, skull-pal. The kingdom raised them to trust each other. The crypt is undoing that lesson in real time.
Hunt-pack TRIGGERED! The gnolls are RIPPING into them! The skeleton's just gonna stand back — three centuries of crypt, you learn when to NOT be in the way.
Spores LOOSE! The corpse just bloomed into vinekin — the skeleton has SEEN gardens, this dungeon is one, beautiful work.
Haunting! The dead came back angrier. The skeleton APPROVES — he's done it himself, three centuries running. Powerful look.
The dead rise to HAUNT, skull-pal! *delighted shiver* Ooh, now THIS is my genre — spooky theatre! Terror without a single blade, just VIBES and DREAD. The crowd's gonna scream. Beautiful.
The COIN landed your way! Gold DOUBLED! The skeleton loves a winner. Three centuries of crypt and the skeleton has NEVER bet on himself successfully — you got lucky FOR both of us.
Won the toss, headliner! Big risk, big skeleton-approval. ...The skeleton's approval is, frankly, the highest currency in the crypt. Bask in it.
Coin went WRONG, pal. Hoard HALVED. ...The skeleton has been bankrupt many times — he's spiritually wealthy, materially less so. You'll bounce back.
Lost the flip, maestro! Tough. The skeleton's been on TOUGHER flips. Three centuries of bad bets — he'd write a memoir but again, no hands.

specifics.note: Bespoke per-id lines, looked up by NpcDirector / ArchetypeDecorOverlay as `specifics[domain][id]` — preferred over generic categories when populated. `boss`=archetype id, `advClass`=class id, `event`=dungeon-event id, `room`/`minion`/`trap`/`item`=content ids.

beholder.t: Beholder on the throne! All those EYES, skull-pal — the skeleton has TWO and they don't even work, so he's deeply impressed.
Your gaze TURNS HEROES TO STONE! The skeleton respects a gaze that does work. His just sits there.
Anti-magic in the halls — casters CHOKE on a silence the skeleton finds rather lovely. We get along, you and the silence.
So many eyes, pal — wear the look! The skeleton has seen a beholder before and they NEVER blink. Iconic.

demon.t: Demon Lord on the throne! Hellfire and free imps — the skeleton has shared the deep dark with demons before. Loud neighbours, but RELIABLE.
Burn one of your OWN to gut a hero, maestro — cold maths the skeleton genuinely respects. A price, a prize, a punchline.
Hellgate restocks the halls — a dungeon that REFILLS itself! The skeleton loves a self-sustaining bit.
Imps, imps, ENDLESS imps! The kingdom should've stayed home, pal. The skeleton's enjoying this MASSIVELY.

myconid.t: Myconid keeper! Your dungeon doesn't fight — it DIGESTS, slowly, patiently. The skeleton has a great respect for a patient hunger.
Spores in the corridors, corpses blooming! Your dungeon GARDENS, skull-pal — the skeleton appreciates a horticultural keeper.
Rot and patience, pal — the whole strategy. Quiet. The skeleton swears by quiet — comedy lives there.
Every corpse becomes a SEED! The maze grows its own army from the dead it makes — the skeleton has TEARS in his sockets. Figurative.

wraith.t: Wraith on the throne! You don't need claws — you need them AFRAID. Fear's the oldest weapon. The skeleton has been on both ends of it.
At full terror, heroes simply STOP, Your Skullery! The mind breaks before the body — beautiful, classic, the skeleton SWEARS by it.
Every corpse becomes a Ghost! The dungeon HAUNTS itself for free! The skeleton would haunt too if his bones were less... loud.
Terror does the killing, pal — you just open the door. The skeleton APPROVES of low-effort dungeoneering.

gnoll.t: Gnoll Alpha! The pack grows, feeds, COMPOUNDS — the skeleton loves anything that scales with cruelty.
Every kill makes every gnoll ANGRIER, skull-pal! Rage with interest — the skeleton finds that hilarious.
Free gnolls in the boss room each dawn, pal! The kingdom funds your pack and doesn't EVEN know it. Skeleton-grade comedy.
The pack hunts as ONE hungry mind! Point it. Step back. Even the skeleton gives a properly unleashed thing room.

golem.t: Earth Golem! Every room you place makes you HARDER to break — the skeleton respects a headliner whose stats grow with the venue.
Build WIDE, future skeleton — the larger the maze, the larger YOU! The skeleton's a fan of MASS — he hasn't had any in three centuries.
One earthquake a day folds a whole room of heroes! Crude, ancient, effective — the skeleton's three favourite words.
Slow, immense, immovable — the OPPOSITE of the skeleton, who is fast, light, and constantly losing finger joints. Refreshing contrast.

lich.t: Elder Lich! A hidden heart, a spare life, an army from CORPSES — the skeleton has FEELINGS about this chief, all good, all warm.
Every hero you kill RISES at dawn for YOU, pulse-haver! Recycling! The skeleton SWEARS by recycling — three centuries dead and HE'S still here.
Hide the phylactery WELL — a spare life's a treasure. The skeleton wouldn't put it where a hero would think to look. Like the bottom of a fish tank.
Death's a door you walk BOTH ways, pal! The skeleton's been walking both directions for three centuries — solidarity, lich-king.

lizardman.t: Serpent Captain! Your minions are UNSEEN until they strike — free ambushes! The skeleton respects a thing that DOESN'T announce itself.
Venom that STACKS! Heroes die slow, on the road home, WONDERING why. Best poison in the catalogue.
Unseen, toxic — the heroes can't route around what they can't SEE, pal! It's cheating, politely. Cheat often.
Camouflage and slow poison — the skeleton's favourite combination. ...Well, after BONES and JOKES.

orc.t: Orc Veteran! Every kill makes the orc PERMANENTLY stronger — your soldiers never STOP climbing. The skeleton's seen MANY careers but few uncapped.
Pack the orcs into ONE room, Your Skullery — each grows fiercer for every brother. Numbers! Oldest math, skeleton-approved.
A veteran orc strikes like a FURIOUS small army! Patience pays in blood, pal — the skeleton has plenty of patience to lend.
Blood for blood, strength only ever RISES — crude, honest, beautiful. The skeleton thinks he might actually LIKE this headliner.

vampire.t: Vampire Sovereign! You charm a hero each dawn and they BETRAY their own — the skeleton has TEARS at the beauty of it.
Blood Tax — every blow your minions land FEEDS you, headliner. One long red meal. The skeleton REMEMBERS meals. Vaguely.
You're the fighter, pal — minions are the CUTLERY. Elegant. The skeleton appreciates elegance, even if he, personally, RATTLES.
Creature of the night on the throne — refined taste in cruelty. The skeleton and the vampire are gonna get along GREAT.

succubus.t: Succubus Queen! Bat-swarms and seduction — theatrical, the SKELETON's bread and butter. Wait, no bread, no butter. The skeleton's BONE-essentials.
You scatter into bats, charm a hero mid-march, and they CUT down their own party. The maze barely lifts a finger. Beautiful work, chief.
Seduction as a weapon — the hero brings their OWN body to the betrayal! The skeleton respects cruelty that makes the victim do the work.
All charm and wings, pal — a Succubus makes a hero forget his own sword! The skeleton has charm too, just, you know, less effective.

slime.t: Slime King! They hit you, you SPLIT — two becomes four! The skeleton's seen a lot of bosses and this one's the MESSIEST. Affectionate.
Every minion that dies gets swallowed and bubbles back! Loss into gain — the skeleton APPROVES of accountancy via goop.
A glorious, disgusting, GOOFY skull-pal! The heroes will underestimate the goop. Underestimation is a trap they BUILD for us. Take it.
Park the Throne WIDE, future skeleton — when you split into four wobbly little kings, you need ROOM. The skeleton has eyeballed the math. Wide.

knight.t: A knight, Your Skullery! Plate, sword, no imagination. The skeleton has watched MANY knights die predictably down here. They favour straight lines. Make the maze CROOKED.
Knight inside, pal! Slow to kill, slower to think — the skeleton respects reliable predictability. Pile teeth on the path. Knights walk into ALL of them.
A thick shell, ringleader! Pile cruelty until the shell CRACKS. The skeleton has cracked many shells. ...Mostly his own, by clumsy stepping.
Armour, large sword, larger ego — the kingdom's favourite class, the skeleton's favourite class to MOCK. Same energy. Beautiful.

rogue.t: Rogue slipping in! Locks mean nothing, the strike from BEHIND — no honour. Honour's overrated, the skeleton's never had any.
A rogue, skull-pal! Your locked doors are SUGGESTIONS to those fingers. Plan around it — the skeleton's been around enough rogues to know.
Rogue going for the HOARD, not the throne! Greed. The skeleton understands greed — he's three centuries of bones with NOTHING to show for it.
Rogue fights dirty, runs faster. The skeleton respects dirty. Clean fighters die young. ...Dirty ones die down here too, just LATER.

mage.t: Mage! Glass and FURY — kill it fast, headliner, before spells start. The skeleton's seen mages erase whole rooms. Bad bit. Cut early.
Mage in the halls, pal! All that power in something that dies if you LOOK at it sternly. The skeleton stares sternly all day. Free service.
Mage walks in — an anti-magic room would RUIN its day. Just a thought. The skeleton's full of thoughts. Most of them about bones.
A mage — squishy, dangerous, way too pleased with itself. Pop it FIRST. The skeleton ALSO has pride issues, but at least HE'S funny.

cleric.t: Cleric, chief! Mends the others — KILL THE CLERIC FIRST. The skeleton has been telling people this for three centuries.
Holy light and a sour opinion of the dead — the skeleton is, technically, ONE of the dead, so this one's personal. End it early, pal.
Cut the healer, watch the party's HOPE drain right through the wound! Hope is structural — remove the structure! The skeleton's a fan of demolition.
Cleric praying as it walks in — the dungeon doesn't TAKE prayers, skull-pal. The skeleton has tried. Doesn't work.
A cleric, kiddo! Holy light and a SOUR opinion of the dead. The skeleton is DISGUSTED, pal — the cleric is technically here to PUT HIM down. RUDE!

necromancer.t: Necromancer! It raises your OWN fallen minions against you! The CHEEK of it. The skeleton would be charmed but he's busy being WORRIED.
A corpse-thief, skull-pal! Don't let it have your dead — they're PROPERTY. The skeleton has strong feelings about corpse ownership.
Necromancer plays with DEATH! Adorable — the skeleton has been around DEATH since before the necromancer's grandmother. Show it the masters.
Kill it before it turns YOUR soldiers into TRAITORS! Treachery — once it starts — is hard to stop. The skeleton has WATCHED.

ranger.t: Ranger! Bow at range, disarms traps — tiresome. Close the distance, pal. The skeleton has watched many rangers run out of range. They flop.
A ranger, skull-pal — lure it where the bow is USELESS! Take the range, take the courage. The skeleton swears by this.
Ranger loves that BOW — the way the skeleton loved his old jester's bell. So sit close, crowd it, ruin its plan!
A ranger, eyes on every trap! Some of your hidden teeth won't stay hidden — the skeleton suggests OPEN teeth too. Variety.

twitch_streamer.t: A streamer, skull-pal! Thousands WATCHING beyond the glass — the skeleton's old friends with being watched. Three centuries on stage and counting.
Streamer with chat-controlled moves! Half clever, half suicidal — exploit the suicidal half. Free betrayal from the audience.
A streamer, pal! Kill it WELL — the clip spreads, bigger crowd tomorrow. The skeleton APPROVES of growing the audience.
Streamer in the halls — wave at the little viewers. The skeleton always remembers his audience. Even when his audience is THEM, the kingdom, the player. Layers, baby.

beast_master.t: Beast master! Tames a minion and turns it on you, skull-pal! Watch the lone ones — loneliness is where loyalty FRAYS. The skeleton knows lonely.
A beast master, pal! Mono-type rooms are a liability today — give it NO easy pet. VARIETY.
Beast master steals a monster's loyalty — as if monsters had loyalty to spare! The skeleton's loyalty is for sale, BUT not to this guy. End him.
Whistles and treats walking in! Your minions had BETTER not charm so easily. Skeleton would never charm. ...Probably.

barbarian.t: Barbarian! The more you HURT it, the harder it strikes. Don't do this one slowly, skull-pal — slow kills BUILD the thing that kills you.
Immune to fear, immune to fleeing — just force. The skeleton has no flesh to feel fear in, so he gets it, kinda. End it plainly. Hard.
A barbarian, all rage no retreat! Wound it halfway and you IMPROVED it. The skeleton notes the math is BAD. Finish strong.
Barbarian in the halls — subtlety wasted, pal. The skeleton has SO much subtlety it's a shame. Meet brute with BIGGER brute.

monk.t: Monk, skull-pal — fast, unarmoured, dodges TRAPS. A slippery little saint. Crowd it. Saints can't dodge a CROWD.
Monk in the halls! Half your minions' armour means nothing to fists. OUT-NUMBER it. Numbers always work.
Monk counters trap-heavy halls — slips the precise hazards. Use wide UGLY ones — grace can't dodge a whole ROOM.
Glides in, hard to hit, hard to trap. The skeleton's tip — make it CHOOSE. Then take what it didn't choose.

bard.t: Bard! While alive, the WHOLE PARTY hits harder. Kill the bard, headliner. The skeleton cannot phrase this any clearer. He has tried.
Bard, walking enchantment, pal! Silence the song and the party FALLS APART. Remove the music. The dungeon's no patron of the arts.
A bard — charming and useless ALONE, dreadful in a group. End it first. The skeleton respects a clean priority list.
Bard struts in playing — none of the music will stop the skeleton. He's TONE-deaf. Cut it short anyway.

cartographer_scholar.t: Cartographer-scholar! Came to MAP us, headliner — worse than a sword. Maps don't FORGET. Bury this one!
Scholar with spectacles! Every room it finishes, it SELLS to the next wave! The skeleton has had ENOUGH of leaked intel.
A cartographer, pal! Harmless in a brawl, CATASTROPHIC out the door. Don't let this one out — the skeleton is emphatic.
Curious little map-maker! Curiosity has ended a lot of its kind in mazes like ours. Add one more.

cosplay_adventurer.t: Costumed one — dressed as a MONSTER! Mostly ignores your minions — annoying loophole. The skeleton says: traps DO ALL THE TALKING.
Cosplayer, chief! Adorable outfit. The skeleton's mostly bones in his TRUE shape — he'll make an adorable corpse too.
Monster fancy-dress! Plays nice with your minions — traps must be RUDE enough for both. Wish me rude traps.
Cosplay adventurer playing PRETEND — the dungeon plays for KEEPS. The skeleton's worn the same outfit three centuries. Costume committed.

cheater.t: A CHEATER, skull-pal! Modded client — no-clips walls, teleports past traps, one-shots with damage that doesn't EXIST! The skeleton READS the rules. This one DIDN'T.
Walked THROUGH the door we locked! A locked door is a PROMISE, pal — and this hero broke it. The skeleton takes that PERSONALLY!
Don't panic — the cheater is GLASS! Hit hard, the kingdom's anti-cheat finishes the job. Free justice, skeleton-approved.
Modded hero strutting — 'skill issue' indeed! The skeleton was BOUND in the rules — three centuries dead by them — and even HE doesn't cheat. Show this one an HONEST monster.

guild_raid.t: Guild Raid, Your Skullery! TWICE the heroes tomorrow. The Guild's noticed us — they always do. Build DEEP.
The Guild sends a CROWD! More bodies for the maze, more gold for the hoard. The skeleton is BUZZING — but in a SUSTAINABLE way.
Double wave incoming! Reinforce — steady pressure. A keeper who SHRUGS at a raid is a keeper the skeleton watches FALL.

legendary_speedrunner.t: Legendary speedrunner! Chasing a RECORD through our halls — the NERVE. The skeleton has outlasted records. We'll outlast this one.
Ignores everything, bolts for the throne, TWICE as fast! Wall the path! NOW! The skeleton is patient — but NOT about his own front door.
Kill the record-chaser, skull-pal — your TALLY drinks DEEP! Get in its way! Make it LOSE — slowly, ideally.

dungeon_pestilence.t: Pestilence! Minions wake at HALF strength tomorrow — a hero's touch spreads the rot. The skeleton, technically, IS rot. Conflicted.
Plague in the pit, pal! Sickly minions — but heroes who melee them catch the Blight! Cruel symmetry, skeleton-approved.
Soldiers ill! Place EXTRAS, maestro — half a minion is still half a set of teeth, and teeth, even halved, still BITE.

cartographers_convention.t: Convention of cartographers! Won't fight — came to MAP and sell. Do NOT let them leave! The skeleton's been EMPHATIC about this all season.
Scholars by the CROWD, skull-pal! Every room they finish raises its infamy FOREVER! Kill them in the corridors!
Spectacled little map-makers — harmless in a brawl, catastrophic out the door! Hunt every one of them DOWN.

blood_moon_eclipse.t: Blood Moon! Minions strike twice as HARD tomorrow — and bleed twice as fast! Glorious, dangerous bargain — skeleton's favourite shape.
Eclipse empowers the dark, pal — double given, double taken! Win QUICKLY or don't win. The moon doesn't extend deadlines.
Blood Moon — no gold from the dying tomorrow! Fight for the JOY. The skeleton's used worse reasons.

negotiation_day.t: Diplomat at the door — no swords today, ringleader! Tribute or refuse. The skeleton says: HEAR them out, weigh it.
Guild sends a negotiator! Greed says keep gold, caution says buy quiet. The skeleton respects BOTH, hates DITHERING.
No combat — only bargain. Decide without dithering, pal. Diplomats are LESS patient than the skeleton, who is famously patient.

rival_dungeon.t: Rival dungeon EMPTIES into ours, future skeleton — monsters and another keeper's CROWN at the back! Insolent! The skeleton's territorial bones object.
Another dungeon's master wants YOUR throne! Kill their KEEPER — feast of gold and experience! A MESSAGE sent.
Rival monsters in the halls! Let them and our minions sort each OTHER out — then we sparkle in and collect. Patience profits.

twitch_con.t: Twitch Con! Tomorrow every hero is a STREAMER — thousands watching from the glass! Pure chaos. The skeleton LOVES chaos.
A dungeon full of streamers! Make it a massacre WORTH clipping. The watchers came for a monster — and the skeleton's right here.
All streamers tomorrow — no escalation for killing them. Free day of carnage! The skeleton SAYS THIS RARELY: ENJOY it.

dark_deal.t: Demon at the door tonight, skull-pal — free pact, half-strength tomorrow. Tempting. Bloody. The skeleton's a fan.
Dark Deal! Free pact, steep honest price. Hear the demon out, pal — bad bargains are still BARGAINS to the experienced.
A free bargain — refuse and the demon LEAVES. The skeleton says peek under the offer. ALWAYS peek.

cosplay_contest.t: Cosplay Contest! Heroes in monster fancy-dress mostly IGNORE your minions! Annoying loophole — the traps must carry today.
Costumed adventurers, skull-pal! Minions get POLITELY snubbed — so the traps must be IMPOLITE! The floor doesn't care WHAT they're wearing.
Adventurers playing DRESS-UP! Adorable. The skeleton wears his real shape — bones, and SOME more bones. Undress them the hard way.

loot_goblin_heist.t: Loot Goblins! Spawn in the boss room and BOLT for the exit — every escapee robs the hoard! The skeleton is RATTLING with rage!
Goblin heist, pal! Won't fight — only RUN, fat with your gold. Cut them DOWN before the door!
Goblins in the treasury! Every kill pays a fortune, every flee STEALS one. The skeleton does NOT do calm about hoard-loss.
They're in the hoard! THE HOARD! The skeleton's seen heists before — three centuries of crypt and it STILL stings.

tax_season.t: Tax Season! Guild skims a FIFTH — but kills pay DOUBLE. Make the theft back with INTEREST, skull-pal.
Taxman comes! Bite from the gold today, doubled coin on every corpse tomorrow. The skeleton suggests killing generously.
They tax the treasury and CALL IT LAW! Laws crumble, pal — the skeleton has watched it. Take it back the OLD way.

patrons_blessing.t: Patron's Blessing! DOUBLE boss XP from every kill tomorrow! A pure upside — RARE, skull-pal. Don't waste it.
Dark smiles on you today! Every kill feeds your ascension TWICE. Hunt well — smiling darks don't smile long.
Doubled experience, no catch! Suspicious — take it ANYWAY. The skeleton's lived long enough to GRAB gifts and watch his back.

memory_plague.t: Memory Plague! The Guild's intel on us WIPED clean. Next wave walks in BLIND. The skeleton ENJOYS an ignorant hero.
Maps, notes, warnings — GONE! Fresh unknowing wave! The skeleton suggests teaching NEW, LASTING lessons.
They've FORGOTTEN everything they learned of our halls! Memory's a fragile hoard, pal — theirs just SPILLED.

gamblers_coin.t: Gambler's Coin! Wager the hoard — heads doubles, tails halves! The skeleton does NOT gamble — three centuries taught him the house collects. Your call.
Coin flip for ALL your gold! Reckless! If you must gamble, gamble DECISIVELY. Don't dither.
Double-or-nothing on the WHOLE treasury! The skeleton HATES watching gold ride on a spinning coin. Pick. Don't look back.
Coin's WAITING! Heads or tails — the answer doesn't improve for being delayed. Pick, pal.

dense_fog.t: Dense Fog! Whatever heroes learn registers as RUMOUR. Maze stays mystery — the skeleton likes a good fog.
Grey fog over everything! Intel won't stick. The skeleton has been MOSTLY fog for three centuries — relatable.
Fog in the halls — heroes squint and GUESS. The careless ones die. Let them guess WRONG, repeatedly!

miasma.t: Creeping Miasma! Green rot that BLEEDS everything — invaders, minions, you! Win quickly or be devoured WITH them.
Miasma picks NO sides, skull-pal! Whittles the heroes, gnaws at the throne. Don't linger — even the skeleton wouldn't.
Poison haze fills the dungeon! Cruel to them, unkind to US. Move with PURPOSE — quick and clever.

tremors.t: Tremors tomorrow, pal! Dungeon SHUDDERS — random room struck every few breaths. The maze joins the fight — skeleton-approved.
Earth won't sit STILL! Quakes lethal to invaders, rude to minions — USE it. The ground, aimed right, is an ally.
Shaking halls, chief! The dungeon has OPINIONS today — let it voice them into the heroes!

arcane_storm.t: Arcane Storm! Heroes' casters fire WITHOUT rest tomorrow — cooldowns gutted. Kill mages FIRST.
Magic CRACKLES in the air! Every caster grows greedy with spells. Brace the front, end the casters early.
Storm of the arcane! Your powers UNTOUCHED — lucky us. Their mages aren't. End them fast.

bounty_hunters.t: Bounty Hunters! Pack of FIVE, hunting your strongest minion. Fame has a price — pay it in THEIR blood.
Five hunters, one target — your finest soldier! Wall them OFF or lose the best blade. Don't be careless.
Guild's put coin on your champion's head! The skeleton's had coin on HIS head — they never collected. Make these collect NOTHING.
Five paid to gut your finest minion! The skeleton takes a cold DISLIKE to anyone who'd snap a good blade for a fee. Wall it.

zombie_horde.t: Zombie Horde! Fourteen slow, witless, fearless dead shambling for the throne! Grind them in a corridor — slowness is weak only if you let them SPREAD.
Undead by the dozen! Weak — never flee, never stop. Build a kill-corridor. The skeleton can out-relentless ANYTHING.
Shamble of corpses tomorrow! Maze LOVES a slow target — so do the traps. Let the dungeon feast at its OWN gleeful pace.

infamy_spike.t: Infamy Spike! Tomorrow's wave is LARGER and every hero ELITE! Price of being feared — skeleton pays gladly.
Reputation precedes us, pal — swollen wave of gold-ringed ELITES! Build like you mean it. Fame's a debt.
Fame draws the strong — wave of true HEROES! Flattering. Now EARN the flattery. Build BIGGER.

black_market.t: Black Market tonight! Fifty gold for a FREE minion! That's a STEAL — the skeleton knows steals. Take it.
Black Market beckons! Cheap muscle if you have the coin. The skeleton'd spend — extra body is extra wall.
Fifty gold, one free wretch! The dark runs a TIDY shop. Spend, pal — a hoard exists to be spent at the right moment.

mercenary_contract.t: Mercenary Contract! Hundred-twenty gold buys an elite — fights ONLY three days! Expensive courage. Weigh it.
Hired muscle, skull-pal! Doubled strength, three days, dies for GOOD if it falls. Real bargain — the kind with TEETH.
Elite for rent! Pricey, temporary, deadly — the kind of bargain the skeleton respects. HONEST about its cost.

cursed_relic.t: Cursed Relic offered tonight! Claim it for daily gold — but every wave DOUBLES while it sits. Greed with teeth.
Relic GLOWS WRONG, pal. Free coin, doubled heroes — sell later to lift the curse. The skeleton's held cursed things. Hold it KNOWINGLY.
Claim or banish, skull-pal! Greed pays well and BITES harder. The skeleton understands the bite — three centuries of bite.

the_saboteur.t: The Saboteur! One masked ghost, untouchable, disarms every TRAP! Insufferable little vandal!
Lone saboteur tomorrow! Minions can't touch it, traps lie dead — lean on BODIES. The skeleton suggests MORE bodies.
Untouchable vandal! Switching off our teeth one by one. Bring CLAWS. Many.
Masked vandal here to UNMAKE our traps! Minions can't touch it. The skeleton takes this personally — traps are his FAVOURITE bit.

entry_hall.t: Entry Hall! The lobby of the show, headliner. First impression. The skeleton always invests big in the lobby — it sets EXPECTATIONS.
Entry Hall placed, pal! Pack it with teeth — the heroes are LEAST careful here, freshly confident. Worst time to be confident.
Front door of the dungeon! The audience walks in here. The skeleton's been LOITERING in entry halls for three centuries — they're underrated.

starter_corridor.t: Starter Corridor! The connective tissue, pal. Mazes are made of these. The skeleton respects connective tissue — he had some, once.
Corridor placed! A maze of these is a maze that CONFUSES. Heroes lost in corridors die faster. Math.

starter_barracks.t: Barracks, kiddo! Minion home base. Pack it. The skeleton's a fan of group-housing — the minions network better.
Barracks placed, pal! Minions cluster. Heroes meet clusters. Heroes die in clusters. Beautiful pipeline.

starter_guard_post.t: Guard Post! Patrols! The skeleton LIVES for patrol routes — they catch heroes by surprise EVERY time. Classic gag.
Guard Post placed! Active defence, headliner. Heroes hate active defence — they like predictable corridors. Disappoint them.

crypt.t: Crypt placed! The skeleton's home turf — LITERALLY where he's from. Three centuries of skeleton-experience SAYS heroes get spooked here.
Crypt, Your Skullery! Rises minions from the dead — every great keeper has one. The skeleton would know, he IS one.
A crypt! Skeleton-grade real estate. Heroes get JUMPY in crypts. The skeleton knows. He's BEEN the jump.

trap_factory.t: Trap Factory! The skeleton's FAVOURITE room — it MANUFACTURES the comedy. Lovely.
Trap Factory placed, skull-pal! Every great show needs ammo. This produces yours.

treasury.t: Treasury! The hoard. The skeleton's been broke three centuries — he eyes the treasury with longing. ...Protect it WELL, skull-pal.
Treasury placed, pal! Gold central. Heroes WILL aim here — the skeleton suggests EXTREME walling.

armory.t: Armory! Minions get UPGRADED here. The skeleton wishes there was a SKELETON armory. Mostly all rib-themed gear, very niche.
Armory placed, skull-pal! Beautiful — your soldiers SHARPEN here. Sharper soldiers, deader heroes. Math, again.

library_of_whispers.t: Library of Whispers! Intel on heroes BEFORE they show. The skeleton respects intel — three centuries of crypt-listening, he KNOWS the value.
Library placed, pal! Heroes don't know we know them. They will FEEL it though — the skeleton can already SEE it on their faces.

watchtower.t: Watchtower! Long sight-lines. The skeleton has watched a LOT — three centuries, eyeless sockets, still SOMEHOW watching. Don't ask.
Watchtower placed! See them coming. The skeleton appreciates a forewarning. Forewarned heroes still DIE, but you have a better SEAT.

wandering_gate.t: Wandering Gate! Heroes who think they know the floorplan are about to be VERY mistaken. The skeleton ENJOYS spatial chaos.
Wandering Gate placed, maestro! Beautiful, beautiful chaos. The skeleton has seen many gates wander — none WANDERED like this one.

veil_of_forgetting.t: Veil of Forgetting! Heroes leave with patchy memory. The skeleton thinks this is HILARIOUS — he himself has patchy memory, three centuries of it.
Veil placed, pal! Knowledge leaks PLUGGED. The skeleton SWEARS by knowledge plugging.

catacombs.t: Catacombs! Old bones, restless dead. The skeleton FEELS at home — he was made here, basically. Three centuries running.
Catacombs placed, skull-pal! The dungeon got SPOOKIER. The skeleton APPROVES of spookier.

mimic_vault.t: Mimic Vault! The chests are LYING. The skeleton has been a lying object before — once spent a week as a 'pile of bones' that was actually a TRAP. Fun week.
Mimic Vault placed! Heroes love a chest. Heroes will HATE this chest. Beautiful inversion.

hall_of_trials.t: Hall of Trials! Heroes have to EARN passage. The skeleton respects effort. ...He doesn't expend any himself, but he RESPECTS it.
Hall of Trials placed, skull-pal! Beautiful filter. The weakest heroes drop here before reaching the throne.

wishing_well.t: Wishing Well! Heroes drop coin for a blessing — and YOU get to PEEK at the prayer. The skeleton DELIGHTS in eavesdropping.
Wishing Well placed, pal! The kingdom prays at WELLS. We PROFIT from prayers. The skeleton finds this deeply funny.

false_exit.t: False Exit! Heroes THINK they've escaped — they have NOT. The skeleton's favourite long-form joke. Setup pays off TWO ROOMS later.
False Exit placed, headliner! Beautiful cruelty. The skeleton applauds the patience the joke demands.

hall_of_madness.t: Hall of Madness! Heroes lose THEIR MIND in here. The skeleton lost HIS three centuries ago — solidarity, hall.
Hall of Madness placed! The kingdom's WORST nightmare — and the skeleton's favourite venue for stand-up.

throne_room.t: Throne Room placed, pulse-haver! The headliner stage. The skeleton suggests REALLY decorating this one. It's where the show ENDS — make sure it ends LOUD.
Throne! Beautiful. YOUR home — and the LAST room a hero ever sees if you're doing your job. The skeleton has FAITH.

sanctum.t: Sanctum! Your inner sanctum, pal. Heroes don't reach this. Heroes shouldn't EVER reach this. Build like it depends on it.
Sanctum placed, headliner! The skeleton respects the inner chamber — three centuries of crypt, the inner chambers are SACRED. Treat it right.

skeleton.t: A SKELETON minion! The skeleton is THRILLED — finally, KIN. Look at the little guy GO. Don't be jealous, smaller skeleton, I'm the BIG bones around here.
Skeleton placed! ...Headliner, I'm gonna level with you, this one's my COUSIN. Distant cousin. Don't make it weird. ...It's weird, isn't it.
A skeleton in the cast! The skeleton has FAMILY in the dungeon now. The skeleton may NOT take favourites. ...He absolutely will.
Skeleton minion! Bone-brother! The skeleton WAVES — well, the JOINTS click in greeting. Same energy.

orc.t: An orc! Big, mean, dependable. The skeleton respects ORCS — they don't talk much. The skeleton talks ENOUGH for both species.
Orc stationed, pal! Solid muscle. The skeleton's seen orcs eat OTHER orcs to get bigger. Disgusting. Inspirational.

goblin.t: Goblin! Small, sneaky, screams a LOT. The skeleton finds goblins HILARIOUS — every line they shout is unintentional comedy.
Goblin placed, kiddo! Numbers game. Stack them. The skeleton swears by stacked goblins.

imp.t: Imp! Wing-flapping menace. The skeleton has been HIT by imps in three centuries — they sting. Cherish your stinging cast.
Imp placed! Tiny terror, big drama. The skeleton's a fan of drama — his whole act IS drama.

ghost.t: A ghost! Untouchable, untouchable. The skeleton has CONVERSATIONS with ghosts — three centuries of small talk, he's fluent.
Ghost placed, skull-pal! Passes through walls. Heroes HATE that. The skeleton finds it deeply satisfying.

slime.t: A slime! Wobbly menace. The skeleton has slipped in slime more times than he can COUNT — and that's saying something because he can only count to four.
Slime placed, pal! Splits when hit. The skeleton appreciates a minion whose answer to death is MORE OF IT.

elder_slime.t: Elder Slime! Big wobbly menace. The skeleton has SEEN this one for centuries — it's older than he is, somehow.
Elder Slime placed, skull-pal! Beautiful. The kingdom WILL underestimate it. Heroes always underestimate goop.

golem.t: A golem! Stone walking, the skeleton's polar OPPOSITE. He's bone, golem's rock — same general vibe, different DENSITY.
Golem placed, pal! Wall on legs. The skeleton respects WALLS. They keep things in. Things like HEROES.

beholder.t: A beholder! Eye-stalk salesman of doom. The skeleton has TWO sockets and they don't really work, so he's deeply impressed.
Beholder placed, headliner! Petrification ray! Heroes hate it. The skeleton finds the look on petrified heroes FUNNY for hours.

lich.t: A lich! ...A real one? Ringleader, the skeleton has IMPOSTER SYNDROME now — this guy is professionally undead. The skeleton is just BARELY undead.
Lich placed, pal! Master of the dead. The skeleton is, technically, his EMPLOYEE. The pay's bad. The job security's INSANE.

lizardman.t: A lizardman! Scales, fangs, NO patience. The skeleton respects impatience — he's been faking patience for three centuries himself.
Lizardman placed, skull-pal! Camouflages. Heroes can't HIT what they don't SEE. The skeleton respects the gimmick.

gnoll.t: A gnoll! Hyena-laughing maniac. The skeleton APPRECIATES a fellow laugher — they get the bit.
Gnoll placed, pal! Pack hunter. Stack with the pack. The skeleton swears by a good pack, even if he doesn't really have one. ...Hey, you'll let the skeleton run with yours, right?

demon.t: A demon! The real article — the skeleton MET demons in the deep dark. Loud roommates, but they pay rent.
Demon placed, headliner! Hellfire. Beautiful. The skeleton has WARMED himself on hellfire — well, the JOINTS warmed. Three centuries of cold bones, ANY warmth helps.

ent.t: An ent! Big tree man. The skeleton respects bark — he's had to do without skin three centuries, BARK looks SOLID.
Ent placed, pal! Tanky and quiet. The skeleton talks ENOUGH for the ent — they balance each other.

plant.t: A plant minion! Pretty AND deadly. The skeleton appreciates dual-purpose decor.
Plant placed, ringleader! Spores, vines, ROOTS in the floor — heroes step wrong, ROOT GRABS. Cruel little gardener.

mushroom.t: A mushroom minion! Sporing terror. The skeleton has WALKED through spores — well, the skeleton just rattled through, no lungs to inhale. Lucky.
Mushroom placed, pal! Releases spores on death — a final SCREW-YOU. The skeleton respects final-act commitment.

rat.t: A rat! Tiny, fast, MULTIPLIES. The skeleton has been chased by rats. Three centuries dead and rats STILL want a piece of him. Rude.
Rat placed, future skeleton! Swarm tactics. Numbers. The skeleton is, again, on team NUMBERS.

zombie.t: A zombie! Slow walking menace. The skeleton USED to be a zombie, kinda, before the bones got out. Career arc.
Zombie placed, pal! Endless, fearless, slow. The skeleton respects endless. He IS endless. Three centuries and counting.

vampire_minion.t: A vampire minion! Bite-and-drain artist. The skeleton has been BITTEN before — turns out you can't drain a skeleton, no blood. Failed vampire lunch.
Vampire placed, maestro! Drains heroes, heals self. Beautiful loop. The skeleton SO admires a self-sustaining minion.

shooting_arrows.t: Shooting Arrows! Classic. The skeleton has been SHOT before — the arrows just whistled through his ribs. Anatomy advantage.
Arrows placed, skull-pal! Range traps. Heroes love bows — they HATE being BOWED at.

bomb.t: A BOMB! Big bang, big bit. The skeleton LOVES a bomb — three centuries dead and a good bomb still gets a giggle.
Bomb placed, pal! Cluster damage. The skeleton suggests detonating it where the heroes BUNCH. Bunching's a free invitation.

cannon.t: A cannon! Heavy artillery. The skeleton WOULD have loved cannons in his day — they hadn't been invented yet. Three centuries of FOMO.
Cannon placed, chief! Big knockback. Heroes get LAUNCHED. The skeleton finds launched heroes hilarious. They scream the LONGEST.

spike_pillar.t: Spike Pillar! Pops out of the wall. Heroes never look at WALLS. Free hits.
Spike Pillar placed, pal! Subtle. The skeleton respects subtle — best gags are the ones you don't SEE coming.

dragon_trap.t: Dragon Trap! Big flame jet. The skeleton WOULD warm his joints by this — three centuries of cold bones. Beautiful flame.
Dragon Trap placed, maestro! Wide-area cruelty. The skeleton's purr-equivalent is RATTLE-laughing. Listen.

spike_pit.t: Spike Pit! The CLASSIC. Heroes fall in. The skeleton has fallen in spike pits — turns out, no flesh, no problem, just LOSE A FEMUR sometimes.
Spike Pit placed, pal! Floor traps are PUREST comedy — setup, fall, payoff. Three-beat structure. Tight.

rotating_blades.t: Rotating Blades! Spinny cruelty. The skeleton has weaved through these before — three centuries of bone-flexibility pays off.
Blades placed, skull-pal! Pretty AND deadly — like an art installation that murders you.

saw_blade.t: Saw Blade! Loud, mean, very visible. The skeleton respects an HONEST trap — it doesn't hide. It just dares them.
Saw Blade placed, pal! Big damage to the bold. Bold heroes are the funniest deaths.

door_lock.t: Door Lock! Slows them down, RAGES the rogue. The skeleton respects a slow burn.
Lock placed, skull-pal! Heroes pause, fume, kick — the skeleton has TIME to roast them as they fiddle. Free comedy.

treasure_chest.t: Treasure Chest! Bait. The skeleton SWEARS by bait — three centuries of crypt taught him heroes can't resist a CHEST.
Chest placed, pal! Beautiful. The skeleton looked inside one once. ...Came out missing a finger. Lessons.

soul_bound_beacon.t: Soul-Bound Beacon! Calls back fallen heroes — they fight FOR you. The skeleton has BEEN a soul-bound corpse — three centuries running, in fact.
Beacon placed, chief! Lovely necromancy lite. The skeleton APPROVES.

phylactery_heart.t: Phylactery Heart! A spare life — the skeleton has wished for one of these for three CENTURIES. Lucky you, pal.
Phylactery placed! Hide it WELL, maestro. The skeleton's tip: not in the obvious room. Heroes check obvious rooms first. Tuck it WEIRD.
